channel.title: IBD LitMonitor - Pregnancy & Reproductive Health
channel.description: IBD literature monitoring: Pregnancy & Reproductive Health

item.title: Clinical utility of blood-based biomarkers in pregnant women with inflammatory bowel disease.
item.description: Expert review of molecular diagnostics - Pregnancy in women with inflammatory bowel disease (IBD) is common, and active disease remains a key driver of adverse maternal and fetal outcomes. Maintaining remission throughout pregnancy is the...

item.title: Quality of healthcare delivery in inflammatory bowel disease pregnancy: a scoping review.
item.description: Inflammatory bowel diseases - Inflammatory bowel disease (IBD) is associated with an increased risk of adverse pregnancy outcomes. The number of individuals affected by IBD during their reproductive years is expected to increas...

item.title: Gasdermin D-Mediated Release of IL-33 Results in Fetal Brain Developmental Abnormalities During Maternal Colitis.
item.description: Advanced science (Weinheim, Baden-Wurttemberg, Germany) - Maternal inflammatory bowel disease (IBD) is associated with an increased incidence of autism spectrum disorder in offspring. The mechanism behind this phenomenon remains largely unknown. Here, we ...

item.title: Disseminated amoebiasis in pregnancy: A maternal near-miss.
item.description: Obstetric medicine - Disseminated amoebiasis in pregnancy, though rare, can be life-threatening and mimic bacterial sepsis or inflammatory bowel disease (IBD). We report a case of maternal near-miss at 18 weeks of gest...
